promise.quote: Here, the view defines the experience. Our guests can be assured that every harbourfront moment, whether from a private suite or the infinity spa pools, is as breathtaking as promised.
promise.name: Michel Chertouh
promise.title: Managing Director

photo.alt: Michel Chertouh, Managing Director of Regent Hong Kong

hero_image.alt: Terrace Suite rooftop terrace at Regent Hong Kong overlooking Victoria Harbour and the illuminated Hong Kong Island skyline at dusk

moments.text: Symphony of Lights from the Lobby Lounge — cocktails as the skyline ignites (Bank of China Tower, IFC, The Peak, Observation Wheel in alignment)
Sunrise from the CEO Suite's harbour-facing terrace
New Year's fireworks & seasonal drone shows through the Lobby Lounge windows
First-light breakfast at Harbourside as the ferries cross the quiet harbour
The secluded east-facing infinity spa pools opening onto Victoria Harbour

best_picks.description: A two-storey rooftop suite with a 2,500 sq ft terrace, private infinity pool, and a 270-degree harbour panorama

image.alt: Regent Hong Kong Presidential Suite rooftop terrace with private pool overlooking Victoria Harbour and the skyline at sunset

Regent Hong Kong Presidential Suite living room with floor-to-ceiling windows framing Victoria Harbour and the Hong Kong Island skyline

best_picks.description: Indoor-outdoor living anchored by a harbour-facing infinity daybed and a 3,200 sq ft terrace built for sunrise

best_picks.description: Two levels with an entertainment room and a generous terrace; panoramic baths facing the harbour

best_picks.description: 1,075 sq ft, capturing the skyline from multiple angles

public_spaces.note: Japanese-Peruvian classics — black cod miso, toro tartare — against the harbour

public_spaces.note: Charcoal-grilled cuts; a handful of tables look straight out over the harbour

Collection of artisan Nesmuk steak knives in a leather case at The Steak House, Regent Hong Kong

public_spaces.name: Harbourside
public_spaces.note: All-day dining and buffet with nearly every table on the harbourfront

public_spaces.name: The Lobby Lounge
public_spaces.note: Floor-to-ceiling skyline views; a front-row seat for the Symphony of Lights and fireworks

public_spaces.name: Pool Terrace
public_spaces.note: An outdoor pool plus secluded east-facing infinity spa pools opening onto the harbour

images.alt: Regent Hong Kong Pool Terrace infinity spa pools at the edge of Victoria Harbour with the Hong Kong Island skyline across the water

promise.quote: Paris does not need to be chased here. From our terraces, the Eiffel Tower and the Arc simply keep you company — at breakfast, at dusk, and long after midnight.
promise.name: John Doe
promise.title: General Manager

moments.text: Champagne at the rooftop's edge as the Eiffel Tower's hourly light show sets Paris sparkling
The panoramic lift's slow reveal of the Arc de Triomphe, floor by floor, on the ride up
Breakfast on the seasonal rooftop — once the founder's private apartment — as the city wakes below
Sunset rosé at Le Jardin du Raphael, with Paris turning gold in a full 360-degree sweep
The antique writing desk of the Eiffel Tower Terrace Suite, framing the Iron Lady in a perfect tableau

best_picks.description: An L-shaped terrace the size of a Parisian apartment, sweeping from the Iron Lady to the Arc and the distant Sacré-Cœur
A bronze staircase, a private terrace, and the rare privilege of dining between the Eiffel Tower and the Arc de Triomphe
A sixth-floor living room with a fireplace, opening onto forty square metres of terrace before Paris's great arch
Seventy square metres named for the hotel's founder, with a private terrace and the Eiffel Tower uninterrupted

public_spaces.name: Le Jardin du Raphael
public_spaces.note: Seventh-floor rooftop restaurant and bar with a 360-degree panorama of Paris — the Eiffel Tower as dining companion (seasonal)

public_spaces.name: Panoramic Lift
public_spaces.note: A theatrical ascent: the Arc de Triomphe emerges through the lift's glass, floor by floor, on the way to the rooftop
